title: Don't show global flags except for podman command
body:
Subcommands should not be showing the global flags.  This causes the important
information to scroll off the screen.

Also fixed a typo on runCommmand (Too many 'm's)

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Admittedly I'm a strange In-Duh-vidual, but I liked having the global flags, despite the scroll on some of the commands.  But not a biggy, I can see why it would be an annoyance for others.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2307) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I added some line breaks to the Usage, so it looks better now.  I think this is ready to merge.
Code LGTM but I like @baude 's head nod on it.
LGTM but I too would like a @baude head nod
